No Poverty.reason: The course content focuses purely on statistical methods without any explicit or inferable link to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course content is entirely focused on statistical methodologies, with no direct or indirect relevance to hunger, food security, or agricultural practices.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course provides foundational statistical inference and data analysis methods, which are crucial tools for health research and managing health risks (Target 3.d), even if not explicitly stated.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops statistical thinking, professional knowledge, and problem-solving skills essential for employment (Target 4.4).
evidence: 奠定相關基礎專業知識(Students will acquire and apply expertise in their respective fields.)
問題解決能力(Students will acquire problem solving skills.)

Gender Equality.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or implicit references to gender equality, women's empowerment, or related issues.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no evidence in the course description or learning outcomes that relates to clean water, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course content does not address energy services, renewable energy, or energy efficiency in any capacity.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops statistical thinking, problem-solving skills, and professional expertise, which are crucial for enhancing economic productivity (Target 8.2) and securing productive employment (Target 8.5).
evidence: 問題解決能力(Students will acquire problem solving skills.)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course provides fundamental statistical inference and data analysis methods, which are essential tools for scientific research and fostering innovation, directly supporting Target 9.5.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course content does not explicitly or implicitly address the reduction of inequalities in any of its dimensions.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus that relates to sustainable cities, communities, or urban development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The statistical inference and data analysis methods taught can be applied to optimize processes, thereby improving resource-use efficiency and reducing waste, aligning with Targets 12.2 and 12.5.

Climate Action.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any direct or indirect references to climate action, climate change, or related environmental issues.

Life Below Water.reason: There is no evidence in the course description or learning outcomes that relates to marine life, ocean health, or sustainable use of marine resources.

Life on Land.reason: The course content does not address terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or sustainable land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any direct or indirect references to peace, justice, or the development of strong institutions.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly aims for students to 'understand and identify... sustainability issues' (Core Competence B) and builds statistical capacity for data analysis, essential for monitoring SDG progress and providing reliable data (Targets 17.18, 17.19).
evidence: using sample data to make generalization about populations
statistical inference and data analysis methods
理解與辨別...永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify...sustainability issues.)
